candidates.name: Leon Aillaud Chavez
roles: Mechanical Engineering Intern – Prototyping & Test Systems
Aerospace Mechanical Engineer
candidates.note: Built inspection fixtures on his OWN 3D printer until the employer funded one; co-founded CADIS; CSU 3D-printed fixed-wing aircraft comp (empennage); MATE ROV submarine + ANSYS FEA. Textbook fit.
candidates.about: I’m a recent Mechanical Engineering graduate from CETYS Mexicali with hands-on experience in design, manufacturing, and analysis. I completed a six-month internship at ROA Pacific Inc. as a Quality Engineer, where I was later hired full-time and helped start a small design area within the company. I designed and built inspection fixtures, worked with vision systems, and developed measurement routines to ensure part quality, initially using my own 3D printer and later one purchased by the company once the value of the work was proven. Alongside my industry experience, I was a member and co-founder of the CADIS group at my university, where we competed in the CSU 3D-Printed Fixed-Wing Aircraft Competition at Cal State LA. Our team fully designed and manufactured a 3D-printed aircraft in SolidWorks under strict requirements, and I focused on the empennage, which strengthened my understanding of aerodynamics, lift, and flight principles. We achieved one of the top placements, reinforcing my interest in aerospace design and structures. I also worked on my capstone project designing a remotely operated submarine for the MATE ROV Competition, where we developed the full system in SolidWorks and performed extensive structural and load analysis in ANSYS, with a strong focus on finite element analysis. Overall, I really enjoy working in teams, designing, testing, learning, and applying engineering theory to real problems, and my main interests are aerospace engineering, mechanical design, and FEA.
candidates.summary:
Resume Evaluation Summary

**Career Stage:** Early-career engineer

**Estimated Experience:** ~1–2 years

**Skills:** Mechanical design, SolidWorks, 3D printing, metrology, automated inspection, injection molding, project management, CAD, Ansys, manufacturing process automation

**Agency:** Demonstrates initiative through hands-on engineering projects, self-driven skill development, and proactive involvement in fixture design and process automation.

**Experience:** Relevant technical experience in mechanical engineering roles, including fixture design, automated inspection programming, and manufacturing support; strong academic foundation with practical project work.

**Entrepreneurship:** Some ownership shown in leading engineering projects and developing solutions from concept to implementation, but no direct evidence of startup or business creation.

**Leadership:** Led hands-on projects (e.g., underwater actuator, aerospace competition empennage), and supported process improvements; member of engineering society, indicating peer leadership and engagement.

**Teamwork:** Active collaboration in university teams and professional settings; experience working cross-functionally in manufacturing and design environments.

**Overall Summary:** Leon is an early-career mechanical engineer with a solid mix of academic excellence and practical, project-based experience. He has demonstrated initiative in both professional and academic settings, with hands-on leadership in technical projects and a strong record of collaboration. While direct entrepreneurial experience is limited, his proactive approach and technical depth are clear. Overall, strong initiative and technical depth with emerging leadership skills.

**Fit for Fast-Moving Startup Teams:** High

intro_email.subject: USAVX — we were impressed by your application
intro_email.body:
Hi Leon,

I'm reaching out from USAVX — we design autonomous unmanned aircraft systems, and your application stood out while we were reviewing candidates.

What stood out to us was that you built inspection fixtures on your own 3D printer until the value was proven — that build-it-and-show-it instinct is exactly how we operate. Your CADIS aircraft project and ROV/FEA work line up closely with the flight hardware we prototype.

Would you be open to a short call this week or next so we can tell you more about the role and hear what you're looking for?

Best,
[Your name]
USAVX

candidates.name: Yixin Zhou
roles: Mechanical Engineering Intern – Prototyping & Test Systems
candidates.note: Formula SAE powertrain — hand calcs + FEA, CAD, 3D printing, CNC. Olin = elite hands-on program. Native-level English.
candidates.about: I’m currently pursuing a degree in Mechanical Engineering and have developed a strong interest in hands-on, applied engineering work, especially where design, analysis, prototyping, and real-world testing all come together. A lot of that has come from my involvement in Formula SAE, where I’ve worked extensively on powertrain components, doing both hand calculations and FEA to guide design decisions. What really draws me to this role is the focus on prototyping and testing mechanical systems, I enjoy the process of taking a design, building it, and refining it through real-world performance and data. I’m comfortable working with CAD, fabrication tools, and prototype systems, and I’m motivated by solving practical engineering problems in a collaborative environment.

candidates.name: Diego Mendoza Madrid
roles: Mechanical Engineering Intern – Prototyping & Test Systems
Aerospace Mechanical Engineer
candidates.note: Össur internship: precision fixtures, tooling, parametric test setups in SolidWorks; CNC + laser cutting; GD&T; Manufacturing Team Lead. Strong hardware, no UAV/SAE.
candidates.about:
I am a Mechanical Engineering student at CETYS Universidad, currently in my final semester, with a strong interest in mechanical design, prototyping, and hardware development. My background combines hands on engineering work with analytical design. During my 1 year internship at Össur I contributed to manufacturing and validation projects for mechanical prosthetic systems, where I designed precision fixtures, tooling, and parametric testing setups in SolidWorks to support assembly processes. This work involved analyzing manufacturing steps, iterating mechanical designs based on test results, and collaborating with technicians to improve reliability and efficiency in the production line.

I am particularly interested in roles where mechanical design connects directly with real hardware. Throughout my academic projects I have worked on the design of automated machines, gear transmission systems, and structural components, including CAD modeling, tolerance analysis, and manufacturing planning using processes such as CNC machining and laser cutting.
What excites me about this role at is the opportunity to work closely with prototype hardware, build and iterate test setups, and contribute to systems related to aviation and autonomous technology. I enjoy environments where engineers design, build, test, and refine physical systems quickly. I am eager to contribute with curiosity, initiative, and a strong hands on approach while continuing to grow in mechanical design and hardware development.

candidates.name: Roberto Monroy Amador
roles: Mechanical Engineering Intern – Prototyping & Test Systems
Aerospace Mechanical Engineer
candidates.note: ~1yr pro manufacturing eng: led plant transfer & global standardization; fixture design; Lean/Six Sigma. Strong but more PROCESS/manufacturing than rapid prototyping. No robotics/UAV/SAE.
candidates.about:
I am a Mechanical and Manufacturing Engineer with a strong passion for building real, functional hardware and turning engineering concepts into practical solutions.

My background combines hands-on manufacturing engineering experience with a deep interest in mechanical design, process optimization, and rapid problem-solving. I have worked on production environments where precision, validation, and efficiency are critical, leading projects involving equipment validation, fixture design, process optimization, and large-scale operational improvements. These experiences trained me to think practically, iterate quickly, and focus on solutions that work outside of simulations and presentations.

What truly motivates me about this opportunity is the chance to work in an environment where engineering goes beyond theory and directly translates into flying hardware. The rapid prototype cycle, the integration of mechanical systems with real world testing, and the challenge of improving designs through iteration strongly align with how I approach engineering: build, test, learn, and improve.

I enjoy working close to the hardware, solving mechanical challenges, and collaborating with multidisciplinary teams to transform ideas into functional systems. I am especially driven by environments where learning speed, adaptability, and ownership matter more than rigid experience requirements.

I am eager to grow into roles with greater technical responsibility, contribute with strong fundamentals and hands-on execution, and be part of a team that is pushing the boundaries of autonomous flight systems.

**Agency:** Demonstrated strong initiative by leading global standardization projects and a full plant transfer, directly driving significant cost savings and operational improvements.

Mechanical Engineering Intern – Prototyp.jd:
### Mechanical Engineering Intern – Prototyping & Test Systems
**Location:** San Francisco, CA – Onsite or Hybrid
 **Team:** Airframe & Mechanical Systems
### About Us
USAvionix is building autonomous aircraft designed to operate in real-world missions. Our Delta platform moves quickly through prototype cycles, combining autonomy, compute, and flight hardware. This work is hands-on, fast-moving, and focused on building systems that actually fly.
### About The Role
We’re looking for a Mechanical Engineering Intern to support prototyping, test setups, and mechanical integration across the Delta platform.
This is a hands-on role. You will design, build, assemble, and iterate physical hardware used for testing and early flight development. You’ll work closely with engineers across autonomy, hardware, and avionics teams, helping turn concepts into real, testable systems.
This role is ideal for someone who learns by building and wants real exposure to aerospace-adjacent hardware development.
### What You’ll Do
- Design and build mechanical test rigs and fixtures
- Create CAD models and assemblies for prototype hardware
- Assemble and integrate mechanical components and housings
- Support ground testing and test setup preparation
- Iterate designs based on test results and feedback
- Help keep prototype hardware organized, accessible, and testable
### What You Should Have
- Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in Mechanical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, or similar
- Comfort with CAD tools such as SolidWorks or Fusion 360
- Interest in hands-on prototyping and physical systems
- Willingness to assemble, disassemble, and iterate hardware
- Curiosity and a bias toward action
### Nice to Have
- Experience with 3D printing, light machining, or composites
- Experience building mechanical fixtures or test setups
- UAV, robotics, or hardware project experience
### Who You Are
You like building real things. You care about how parts fit together and how systems are tested. You’re not afraid to get your hands dirty, learn quickly, and improve designs through iteration.
### Why You’ll Love Working Here
- Real ownership as an intern
- Direct exposure to flight hardware and testing
- Tight feedback loop from CAD to physical systems
- Small team with meaningful impact

Aerospace Mechanical Engineer.jd:
## Aerospace Mechanical Engineer – Airframe & Prototyping
**Location:** San Francisco, CA – Onsite or Hybrid
 **Team:** Airframe & Mechanical Systems
### About Us
USAvionix is building autonomous aircraft that actually operate in the real world. Our Delta platform moves through rapid prototype cycles, starting with electrically powered, ducted-fan aircraft. This work matters beyond a demo. It matters in mission contexts.
### About The Role
We’re looking for an early-career Aerospace Mechanical Engineer to help design, build, test, and iterate airframes and mechanical subsystems.
 You don’t need prior aircraft experience. What we value most is **strong fundamentals, CAD ability, curiosity, and the drive to turn ideas into hardware that flies**.
This role is hands-on. You’ll be involved in CAD, prototyping, test setups, and flight support.
### What You’ll Do
- Design and iterate mechanical and airframe components for electrically powered autonomous aircraft
- Create and maintain CAD models and assemblies
- Build prototypes with 3D printing, composites, and basic machining
- Support ground testing and flight test preparation
- Build test rigs, fixtures, and simple tooling
- Analyze test results and failures and apply learnings to next iterations
- Collaborate with autonomy, avionics, and systems engineers
### What You Should Have
- Degree in Mechanical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, or equivalent hands-on experience
- Strong mechanical fundamentals and problem solving
- Experience or comfort with CAD (SolidWorks, Fusion 360, or similar)
- Passion for building and testing real hardware
- Ability to learn airframe and flight concepts quickly
- A bias toward action in a fast prototype environment
You don’t need previous aircraft or airframe experience. If you can learn fast and ship parts that work, you’ll thrive here.
### Nice to Have
- UAV, RC, aerospace, or flight project experience
- Experience with 3D printing, composites, CNC
- Exposure to structural analysis or basic FEA
- Test rig or flight test support experience
### Who You Are
You are a doer. You turn CAD into parts. You learn by building and testing. You are not afraid of failure, you learn from it. You want to grow into bigger technical ownership over time.
### Why You’ll Love Working Here
- Meaningful ownership early in your career
- Tight loop from CAD to prototype to flight
- Small, focused team with direct impact
- Opportunity to work across mechanical systems, structures, and flight test

Hardware / Embedded Systems Intern – AI.jd:
### Hardware / Embedded Systems Intern – AI Test & Integration
**Location:** San Francisco, CA – Onsite or Hybrid
 **Team:** Autonomy, Hardware & AI Systems
### About Us
USAvionix is building autonomous aircraft designed to operate in real-world missions. Our Delta platform combines autonomy, onboard compute, and high-speed flight hardware. We move quickly from idea to test to iteration, with a strong bias toward systems that actually work outside the lab.
### About The Role
We’re looking for a Hardware or Embedded Systems Intern to support AI system bring-up, testing, and integration across the Delta platform.
This is a hands-on role focused on building and validating real systems. You will work with Raspberry Pi based platforms, cameras, and AI accelerators to help bring up test setups for Delta AI and Phalanx AI modules.
You will not be training models or building perception algorithms from scratch. You will be helping make sure the hardware and software stack runs reliably and is easy to test and iterate on.
### What You’ll Do
- Bring up and configure Raspberry Pi based systems
- Integrate cameras and basic capture pipelines
- Build and maintain AI test harnesses and bench setups
- Support testing of Delta AI and Phalanx AI modules
- Document setup steps, configurations, and test procedures
- Assist in debugging basic integration and stability issues
### What You Should Have
- Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, Mechatronics, Robotics, or similar
- Comfort working with Linux based systems
- Experience or interest in embedded systems or robotics
- Familiarity with Python or C++
- Willingness to debug and learn through hands-on testing
### Nice to Have
- Experience with Raspberry Pi or similar SBCs
- Camera or sensor integration experience
- Exposure to robotics, autonomy, or AI systems
- Experience with basic electronics and wiring
### Who You Are
You like making systems work. You’re comfortable when things are half-built and messy. You enjoy bringing hardware and software together and learning by doing.
### Why You’ll Love Working Here
- Real exposure to AI and autonomous systems
- Hands-on ownership of test and integration work
- Tight feedback loop from bench testing to flight systems
- Opportunity to grow into deeper technical ownership over time

AI Engineer.jd:
# AI Engineer - Edge Autonomy (LLM Focus)
**Location:** San Francisco, CA - Onsite (Flexible)
**Team:** USAvionix
**Type:** Full-time

We’re building a new generation of autonomous ISR aircraft capable of sensing, deciding, and acting in milliseconds, without relying on the cloud.
Your work will power the onboard AI agents that fly, detect, and coordinate missions in real time.

## What you’ll do
- Build and fine-tune LLM systems that power onboard decision-making for autonomous aircraft.
- Design agentic workflows that coordinate three core AI agents onboard:
    - Operator Agent – mission brain, planning and arbitration
    - Pilot Agent – real-time flight and camera control
    - Intel Agent – perception and verification
- Develop low-latency communication protocols between these agents to ensure reliable, deterministic behavior during flight.
- Build prompt orchestration and retrieval pipelines for situational awareness and mission control.
- Deploy optimized models on embedded hardware that handle variable telemetry in real time.
## What we’re looking for
- Strong background in Python, PyTorch, and modern LLM frameworks (e.g., Hugging Face, vLLM, llama.cpp).
- Experience with fine-tuning, RAG, embeddings, or custom model adapters.
- Comfort building from research ideas to production-ready code.
- Experience working with real-time or mission-critical systems.
- Strong understanding of edge inference (quantization, optimization, GPU/CPU/NPU).
## Nice to have
- Familiarity with small language models (1–3B), grammar-constrained decoding, or state-space models.
- PX4/ArduPilot/MAVLink or drone systems.
- Vision systems (OCR, lightweight detectors) or real-time telemetry handling.
- Experience with 3D simulation (Three.js, Cesium, or similar).

These agents don’t live in the cloud, they fly onboard, coordinating in real time to search, verify, and adapt.

## **Why USAVX**
USAvionix builds autonomous systems that act faster and smarter than the threat. In this role, you’ll own one of our core technologies - shaping how autonomy is coordinated, deployed, and trusted in the field.

Strategic Projects Intern.jd:
# Strategic Project Intern 
**Location:** San Francisco, CA - Onsite (Flexible)

USAvionix is building a new generation of autonomous ISR aircraft capable of sensing, deciding, and acting in milliseconds without relying on the cloud.
We are looking for a Strategic Projects Intern to support leadership across internal initiatives, marketing coordination, and company projects.
You will work directly with the founders helping coordinate initiatives, organize information, and ensure projects remain on track.

- Strong communication and writing skills

- Comfortable supporting coordination and administrative tasks

Operations Intern.jd:
Operations Intern - Scheduling, Vendor Management & Logistics

**Location:** San Francisco, CA - Onsite (Flexible)
**Team:** USAvionix
**Type:** Full-time

USAvionix is building a new generation of autonomous ISR aircraft capable of sensing, deciding, and acting in milliseconds, without relying on the cloud.

We are seeking an Operations Intern to help drive the administrative and operational backbone of our fast-growing aerospace programs. You will work directly with the operations team and leadership to ensure smooth day-to-day execution across supply management, scheduling, and vendor coordination.

### What you’ll do
- Oversee and optimize supply chain and inventory management processes
- Coordinate scheduling for internal teams, meetings, and project milestones
- Manage vendor relationships, contracts, and deliverables
- Track deliverables, update internal documentation, and keep the moving parts around hardware and software builds from drifting
- Support procurement, order tracking, and logistics
- Assist with documentation, reporting, and process automation

### What we're looking for
- Final-year undergraduate or master’s student, or recent graduate in business, operations, or related field
- Experience or coursework in supply chain, logistics, or operations management
- Strong organizational and time management skills
- Proficiency with spreadsheets, scheduling tools, and workflow automation
- Excellent communication and problem-solving abilities
- Self-starter who thrives in a dynamic, high-autonomy environment

## Why USAVX
USAvionix builds autonomous systems that act faster and smarter than the threat. In this role, you’ll help shape the operational foundation that enables our technology to scale and succeed in the field.
